title: Stomach
body_html:
The stomach is a J-shaped organ that lies below the diaphragm. It is a muscular sac with two openings. The cardiac opening superiorly and the pyloric opening inferiorly.

It has two curvatures. The lesser curvature is attached to lesser omentum. The greater curvature is attached to greater omentum.

The pyloric sphincter is a true anatomical sphincter (unlike the cardiac sphncter/LES/GOJ) and lies at L1 level in the recumbent position.

Arterial and nerve supply to the stomach
Stomach supplyStructure
Arterial supplycoeliac trunk:
  • Left gastric artery
  • Splenic artery
  • Common hepatic arteries
Parasympathetic nerve supplyvagus nerves
Sympathetic nerve supplycoeliac plexus
T5-T9
Lymph drainagecoeliac nodes

Anatomical relations to the stomach
Anterior relationsPosterior relations
  • Anterior abdominal wall
  • Diaphragm (mainly anterior and superior)
  • Gall bladder
  • Greater omentum
(starts at the anterior inferior part of the stomach)
  • Left lobe of liver
  • Pancreas
  • Lesser sac
  • Left adrenal gland
  • Left kidney
  • Spleen
  • Splenic artery
  • Transverse mesocolon

    Diagram illustrating the nerve supply of the stomach. Note how the left vagus lies on the anterior wall near the GOJ
